commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Needs human/contributor handling because live Telegram proof is missing and the remaining topic-boundary plus compatibility rollout decision is not a safe automated repair.; Needs attention: The diff tightens a message-routing security boundary, but the Telegram matcher currently lets explicit different-topic sends pass as same-context.; Review finding: [P1] Keep explicit Telegram topics scoped — extensions/t... (sha=a03e12db07406258300c9000e406add40b4ac404)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes same-provider `message` cross-context sends from opt-out to opt-in, adds Telegram chat-level context matching, updates focused tests/docs/config help, and carries an exec-policy merge-resolution change.

PR surface: Source +49, Tests +188, Docs +3. Total +240 across 15 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: PR head compares only Telegram chat IDs and its test expects a different topic in the same chat to match the current topic context. I did not run a live Telegram transport repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs human/contributor handling because live Telegram proof is missing and the remaining topic-boundary plus compatibility rollout decision is not a safe automated repair.

target.title: Deny same-provider cross-context message sends by default
target.body:
## Summary

Deny same-provider cross-context `message` tool sends by default. A tool call bound to one conversation can still reply to the current conversation, but sending/uploading into another conversation on the same provider now requires explicit opt-in via:

```json5
{
  tools: {
    message: {
      crossContext: {
        allowWithinProvider: true
      }
    }
  }
}
```

The legacy broad `tools.message.allowCrossContextSend=true` escape hatch is preserved.

## What Problem This Solves

Cross-provider sends were already denied by default, but same-provider cross-context sends were allowed unless `allowWithinProvider` was explicitly set to `false`. That permissive default makes it too easy for work/approval in one Discord thread to accidentally drive an externally visible message send into another thread on the same provider.

This PR makes current-conversation delivery the safe default while preserving an explicit compatibility opt-in for installations that intentionally bridge conversations.

## Duplicate / overlap check

I checked upstream issues/PRs and local source for exact overlap before opening this PR. I did not find an exact duplicate for changing the same-provider cross-context default.

Adjacent but not duplicate:

- #74799: per-session runtime envelopes; useful substrate, but not this default policy change.
- #91800: external content provenance/policy-hook style; adjacent, not cross-context send authorization.
- #77442 / #88803 / #89949 / #54531: delivery route/origin persistence; adjacent routing work, not this message tool default.

## Panel review / scope revision

A 3-lane consult review recommended a narrow first PR rather than a broad runtime approval resolver. The revised scope is intentionally limited to the existing `enforceCrossContextPolicy` seam:

- no live patch / no gateway activation
- no cron/webhook changes in this PR
- no rewrite of Discord routing/session compaction
- preserve ordinary replies to the current conversation
- keep explicit config opt-in for existing cross-context workflows

## Changes

- Change `allowWithinProvider` default from permissive (`!== false`) to explicit opt-in (`=== true`).
- Update policy tests for default same-provider denial and explicit same-provider opt-in.
- Update config help text and FAQ wording to document that cross-context messaging is blocked by default, including same-provider conversation hops.

## Evidence

Latest amended/rebased head: `e0e4259e40c283f986d790686e929463628ed77f`.
Current base used for local validation: `openclaw/openclaw@2920dc32820cb2add798cbca294131a0017dbc70`.

Focused validation after fixing the broad shard failures:

```bash
node scripts/test-projects.mjs test/scripts/lint-suppressions.test.ts -- --run
node scripts/test-projects.mjs \
  src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.core-send.test.ts \
  src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.context.test.ts -- --run
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
pnpm check:test-types
git diff --check
```

Observed result:

```text
lint-suppressions.test.ts: 3 tests passed
message-action-runner.core-send.test.ts: 11 tests passed
message-action-runner.context.test.ts: 26 tests passed
core prod tsgo: passed
core test tsgo: passed
pnpm check:test-types: passed
git diff --check: passed
```

The failing broad shard cases are now covered directly:

- same-provider message-tool-only sends are denied by default before durable delivery;
- explicitly opted-in same-provider cross-context sends still preserve required durable delivery;
- dry-run UI handoff tests now assert default denial for same-provider conversation hops;
- direct/local chat mismatch cases now assert default denial while same-conversation sends remain allowed;
- current-main lint suppression allowlist drift is refreshed for the rebased tree;
- current-main `exec-policy` generic layer merge preserves `base` fields while satisfying TS boundary checks.

Earlier focused assertion check also passed in an isolated worktree:

```bash
node_modules/.bin/tsx /tmp/test-cross-context-policy.ts
# cross-context policy focused assertions passed
```

## Rollout note

This is a behavior-tightening default. Existing deployments that intentionally send to other conversations on the same provider should set `tools.message.crossContext.allowWithinProvider=true`.
target.author: anyech

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
